title: Setting Up Your First React App
description: Get React running on your machine in minutes.

slides.title: Modern React Setup with Vite 🏗️
slides.content:
# The Fastest Way to Start

**Create React App** was the older officially supported tool (it is now deprecated and no longer the best/recommended way). The modern approach is **Vite** — it’s faster, uses native ES modules, and has zero-config hot reload.

## Steps

1. Make sure you have **Node.js** installed (version 18 or above).
2. Open your terminal and run:
   ```bash
   npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template react
   ```
3. Then navigate into the project and start it:
   ```bash
   cd my-app
   npm install
   npm run dev
   ```

Your app will be live at http://localhost:5173. That’s it — you’re ready to code!

quizData.question: What command creates a new React app using the modern approach (Vite)?
quizData.explanation: The correct modern command is `npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template react`. Create React App is deprecated; Vite is now the recommended way because it’s much faster and uses native ES modules.

slides.title: Common Pitfall ⚠️
slides.content:
## Node.js Version

If you get an error, make sure Node.js is installed and up-to-date (version 18 or above for Vite). Run `node -v` in your terminal to check.
